prompt:
Passage 1
A regional facilities department recently changed how it handles requests for room temperature adjustments. Previously, employees called the front desk when a work area felt too warm or too cold. The front desk then passed the message to maintenance staff. During busy periods, some requests were delayed or repeated because there was no single record of the issue.
The department introduced an online comfort request form. Employees now enter the room number, the concern, the time the issue was noticed, and whether the problem affects a large area or a single workspace. Maintenance staff review the requests several times a day and check whether multiple reports are coming from the same area.
Managers say the new process helps identify patterns, such as repeated complaints from one section of the building. It also allows maintenance staff to respond in a more organized way.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Employees should stop reporting temperature problems.
choices.B: An online request form improved how temperature concerns are tracked and handled.
choices.C: The front desk now repairs heating and cooling equipment.
choices.D: Room temperature problems cannot be solved.

explanation: The passage focuses on how the online comfort request form made temperature concerns easier to record, track, and respond to.

prompt:
Passage 2
NOTICE
Beginning January 8, employees who use the shared print room must clear paper jams only if they have been trained to do so. Employees who are not trained should place an “out of service” sign on the machine and contact Office Support.
The updated procedure was created after several printers were damaged when users pulled stuck paper from the wrong area. The purpose is to reduce equipment damage and make sure problems are handled safely.
Office Support will offer short printer-care training sessions next week for employees who regularly use the print room.

What is the purpose of the notice?

choices.A: To announce a new printer-use procedure related to paper jams
choices.B: To close the shared print room permanently
choices.C: To require all employees to repair printers
choices.D: To explain changes to office furniture

explanation: The notice announces a procedure for handling printer paper jams safely and avoiding damage.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many companies use incident trend reviews to improve workplace safety. Instead of looking at each incident separately, managers review reports over a period of time to see whether certain types of problems are happening repeatedly.
For example, if several minor hand injuries occur in the same department, the company may review glove use, tool condition, training, or work pace. The goal is to understand why similar incidents continue to happen and what changes may reduce future risk.
Trend reviews are most useful when reports contain accurate details. If reports simply state that an employee “got hurt,” managers may not have enough information to identify a cause. Clear descriptions help turn individual reports into useful safety information.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Incident trend reviews help companies find repeated safety problems and reduce risk.
choices.B: Safety reports should include as little information as possible.
choices.C: Managers should examine incidents only one at a time.
choices.D: Minor injuries do not need to be reported.

explanation: The passage explains that incident trend reviews help companies identify repeated safety problems and reduce future risk.

prompt:
Passage 4
Before automatic irrigation timers became common, many public parks relied on workers to turn sprinkler systems on and off manually. This approach required staff time and could lead to uneven watering if schedules were missed or sprinklers were left running too long.
Automatic timers changed park maintenance by allowing watering schedules to be set in advance. Parks could water grass, plants, and athletic fields during early morning hours when evaporation was lower and fewer visitors were present. Timers also helped reduce overwatering by shutting systems off after a set period.
Modern irrigation systems may include moisture sensors or weather-based controls. Even with these improvements, workers still inspect sprinkler heads, pipes, and coverage patterns to make sure the system works properly.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Automatic irrigation timers improved watering efficiency but still require inspection.
choices.B: Public parks no longer need water.
choices.C: Workers should always leave sprinklers running all day.
choices.D: Irrigation systems are used only inside buildings.

explanation: The passage explains how automatic irrigation timers improved watering schedules while still requiring inspection.

prompt:
Passage 5
A regional call center recently revised its training for handling customer complaints. The previous training focused mainly on finding the correct department or policy. While that information was useful, supervisors noticed that some employees sounded rushed or defensive when customers were upset.
The revised training teaches employees to listen carefully, confirm the customer’s concern, and explain the next step clearly. Employees practice short role-play conversations in which they must respond calmly before transferring the call or entering a service request.
Supervisors report that customer follow-up surveys have improved since the training was revised. They believe customers are more satisfied when they feel their concern was understood, even if the problem cannot be solved immediately.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The call center stopped training employees.
choices.B: Revised complaint training improved how employees communicate with upset customers.
choices.C: Customers are no longer allowed to make complaints.
choices.D: Employees now avoid explaining next steps.

explanation: The passage focuses on how revised training helped call-center employees communicate better with upset customers.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many workplaces use color-coded tags to show the status of equipment. A green tag may show that equipment is ready for use, while a red tag may show that it should not be used until repaired or inspected. Other colors may identify items waiting for cleaning, testing, or supervisor approval.
Status tags help employees make quick decisions. Instead of guessing whether a tool or machine is available, workers can check the tag before using it. This can prevent mistakes, especially in busy areas where several similar items are stored together.
The system works only if tags are kept current. If a repaired item still has a red tag or a damaged item keeps a green tag, employees may receive the wrong message. Regular tag checks help keep the system reliable.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Color-coded tags can help identify equipment status when kept current.
choices.B: Equipment tags are useful only when they are missing.
choices.C: Workers should guess whether equipment is ready.
choices.D: Similar tools should never be stored together.

explanation: The passage explains that color-coded tags help identify equipment status, but only if the tags are current.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many communities maintain public storm shelters in areas where severe weather can occur. These shelters are designed to give residents a safer place to go when conditions become dangerous. They may be located in schools, community centers, or specially built structures.
Clear public information is important because residents need to know where shelters are located, when they are open, and what items they should bring. Some shelters may not allow pets unless they are service animals, and many shelters ask residents to bring medications, identification, and basic personal supplies.
Shelter plans must be reviewed regularly. Contact numbers, building access procedures, and capacity limits can change over time. Keeping information current helps communities respond more effectively during severe weather events.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: Public storm shelters require clear and current information to help residents use them safely.
choices.B: Storm shelters are used only for school events.
choices.C: Residents should avoid learning shelter locations.
choices.D: Severe weather plans never need review.

explanation: The passage explains that storm shelters are useful only when residents have clear, current information about location, rules, and access.

prompt:
Passage 8
An adult education center recently added short goal-setting meetings to its apprenticeship entrance-exam course. Instructors noticed that some students attended class regularly but did not know how to organize study time outside class.
During the meetings, students identify one or two specific goals for the week, such as completing a practice set, reviewing missed questions, or reading for twenty minutes each evening. Instructors help students choose goals that are realistic based on work schedules and family responsibilities.
The center does not expect every student to study the same way. Instead, the goal-setting meetings are meant to help students create a plan they can actually follow. Early feedback suggests that students feel more in control of their preparation.

Why did the center add goal-setting meetings?

choices.A: Some students needed help organizing study time outside class.
choices.B: Students stopped attending class regularly.
choices.C: Instructors wanted to cancel practice sets.
choices.D: The course no longer prepared students for entrance exams.

explanation: The center added goal-setting meetings because some students struggled to organize study time outside class.

prompt:
Passage 8
An adult education center recently added short goal-setting meetings to its apprenticeship entrance-exam course. Instructors noticed that some students attended class regularly but did not know how to organize study time outside class.
During the meetings, students identify one or two specific goals for the week, such as completing a practice set, reviewing missed questions, or reading for twenty minutes each evening. Instructors help students choose goals that are realistic based on work schedules and family responsibilities.
The center does not expect every student to study the same way. Instead, the goal-setting meetings are meant to help students create a plan they can actually follow. Early feedback suggests that students feel more in control of their preparation.

Which weekly goal is mentioned in the passage?

choices.A: Reading for twenty minutes each evening
choices.B: Repairing classroom equipment
choices.C: Writing company policies
choices.D: Avoiding all missed questions

explanation: Reading for twenty minutes each evening is specifically listed as one possible weekly goal.
